conflicts.reason: Compete for DMT1 transporter. Separate by 2-4 hours.
Zinc induces metallothionein which binds and blocks copper absorption. Long-term zinc requires copper supplementation.
Calcium inhibits both heme and non-heme iron absorption by 50-60% at doses above 300mg.
Compete at DMT1. Separate by 2-4 hours for optimal absorption.
Share DMT1 transport. Iron deficiency upregulates manganese absorption and vice versa.
Curcumin chelates iron, reducing absorption. Separate by 2+ hours.
Tannins in coffee chelate iron, reducing absorption up to 60%.
Polyphenols in tea chelate iron, reducing absorption up to 60%.
Risk of serotonin syndrome — a potentially fatal interaction. Do not combine.
Risk of serotonin syndrome. Do not combine.
K2 directly antagonizes warfarin's anticoagulant mechanism.
Berberine inhibits CYP3A4 which metabolizes most statins, increasing statin levels.
Berberine inhibits CYP2C9 and CYP3A4 which metabolize warfarin.
ALA may affect thyroid hormone levels. Separate by 4 hours.
Ashwagandha may increase thyroid hormone levels, affecting dosing.
Bacopa may increase T4 levels.
Charcoal binds NAC, preventing absorption.
Alcohol disrupts the melatonin circadian rhythm and reduces effectiveness.
Ginseng has mild antiplatelet effects that may be additive with anticoagulants.
Ganoderic acids in reishi have mild antiplatelet effects.
Additive sedation — both increase GABA activity.
Additive sedation via GABA-A modulation.

synergies.reason: D increases calcium absorption, K2 directs it to bones instead of arteries.
Magnesium is a required cofactor for both hydroxylation steps of vitamin D metabolism.
Vitamin C converts Fe3+ to Fe2+, increasing non-heme iron absorption 2-3x.
Vitamin C is an essential cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase in collagen synthesis.
PQQ stimulates new mitochondria via PGC-1alpha; CoQ10 fuels them.
Fulvic acid in shilajit enhances CoQ10 levels in mitochondria.
Both feed into glutathione recycling — synergistic antioxidant support.
The 'Ames combo' — synergistic reversal of mitochondrial decay in aging studies.
NAD+ is the substrate SIRT1 needs; resveratrol accelerates SIRT1. Synergistic.
Selenium-dependent deiodinases convert T4 to active T3.
Piperine increases curcumin bioavailability by 2000% by inhibiting glucuronidation.
Vitamin E prevents omega-3 oxidation in the body.
Astaxanthin protects omega-3 fatty acids from oxidation in cell membranes.
B6 enhances zinc absorption and zinc enhances B6 metabolism — bidirectional.
Vitamin C regenerates oxidized quercetin and improves its bioavailability.
Both calming — magnesium blocks NMDA receptors, glycine lowers core body temperature for sleep.
